title: rosa coriifolia subsp. subcanina
intro: Rosa coriifolia subsp. subcanina, also known as the wild rose or dog rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rosa coriifolia subsp. subcanina include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, as well as its ability to support immune function and provide antioxidant protection. Rosa coriifolia subsp. subcanina can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil to harness its medicinal properties.
